---
title: "data_work"
output: html_document
---

# General first data pull 
```{r, include=FALSE}
source("setup.R")

# Download the meta-data for all sites
meta_data <- snotel_info()

# View the first few rows to understand the structure
head(meta_data)

id = 438 # Deadman Hill = 438
# Download data for a specific site and return as a data frame
df <- snotel_download(site_id = id, internal = TRUE)

# View the first few rows of the downloaded data
head(df)

# make this column date objects
df$date <- as.Date(df$date)

# Make columns for day of water year and water year
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    water_year_start = if_else(month(date) >= 10,
                               make_date(year(date), 10, 1),
                               make_date(year(date) - 1, 10, 1)),
    day_of_water_year = as.integer(date - water_year_start) + 1,
    water_year = if_else(month(date) >= 10,
                         year(date)+1,
                         year(date),
  )) %>%
  select(-water_year_start)

df %>% select(date, water_year, day_of_water_year) %>% head()

# view new columns
print(df$day_of_water_year)

# View a water year
library(ggplot2)
df_subset <- df %>% filter(water_year == 2025) # filter df to one year
ggplot(df_subset, aes(x = date)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = snow_water_equivalent, color = "SWE")) +
  geom_line(aes(y = precipitation_cumulative, color = "Precipitation")) +
  labs(
    title = "Snow Water Equivalent and Precipitation – Water Year 2025",
    x = "Date",
    y = "mm of water",
    color = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Function to look at different time periods
compare_mean_swe_by_wyday <- function(
    df,
    swe_col = snow_water_equivalent,
    period1 = c(1980, 2000),
    period2 = c(2001, 2025)
) {
  
  df_proc <- df %>%
    mutate(
      period = case_when(
        water_year >= period1[1] & water_year <= period1[2] ~
          paste0(period1[1], "–", period1[2]),
        water_year >= period2[1] & water_year <= period2[2] ~
          paste0(period2[1], "–", period2[2]),
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(period))
  
  swe_mean <- df_proc %>%
    group_by(period, day_of_water_year) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_swe = mean({{ swe_col }}, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  ggplot(swe_mean, aes(x = day_of_water_year, y = mean_swe, color = period)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
    labs(
      title = "Mean Snow Water Equivalent by Day of Water Year",
      x = "Day of Water Year",
      y = "Mean Snow Water Equivalent",
      color = "Period"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
}

compare_mean_swe_by_wyday(df)
compare_mean_swe_by_wyday(df, swe_col=precipitation_cumulative)
```

# Depth over years, starting in 2003-07-15 
```{r, echo=FALSE}
df %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = date, y = snow_depth)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_x_date(
    limits = c(as.Date("2003-10-01"), NA)
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

# Computations for depth x SWE
```{r}
# getting a better estimate than Mizukami (will just be comparison for what we do)
# density(ps) = denswater(pw) (swe/depth)
# take an average value of the densities over around 15 years (up until 2020)
  # apply it to the last 5 years individually to evaluate how good that average is 
# compare to the 164.7 kg/m^3
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    snow_density = case_when(
      is.na(snow_depth) | is.na(snow_water_equivalent) ~ NA_real_,
      snow_depth <= 0 ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE ~ (snow_water_equivalent / snow_depth) * 1000  # kg/m^3
    )
  )
```

# 15-year graph with trend
```{r, echo=FALSE}
# one fitted avg density line from Dec 1 - Mar 1, Mar 1 - Apr 30 ON TOP of the 15 years 
# one fitted avg density line from Dec 1 - Mar 1, Mar 1 - Apr 30 ON TOP of the years
# ----------------------------
# 1) Keep full water year data (2005–2020)
# ----------------------------
df_0520 <- df %>%
  filter(water_year >= 2005, water_year <= 2020) %>%
  filter(!is.na(snow_density)) %>%
  arrange(water_year, day_of_water_year)

# ----------------------------
# 2) Average density by day-of-water-year across years
# ----------------------------
avg_by_day <- df_0520 %>%
  group_by(day_of_water_year) %>%
  summarise(avg_density = mean(snow_density, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(day_of_water_year)

# ----------------------------
# 3) Define key day-of-water-year cut points (non-leap reference)
# WY starts Oct 1 = day 1
# Dec 1 = 62, Mar 1 = 152, Apr 30 = 212
# ----------------------------
dec1 <- 62
mar1 <- 152
apr30 <- 212

# Fit datasets (only where you want the trend modeled)
fit1_data <- avg_by_day %>% filter(day_of_water_year >= dec1, day_of_water_year <= mar1)
fit2_data <- avg_by_day %>% filter(day_of_water_year >= mar1, day_of_water_year <= apr30)

# Models
fit1 <- lm(avg_density ~ day_of_water_year, data = fit1_data)
fit2 <- lm(avg_density ~ day_of_water_year, data = fit2_data)

# ----------------------------
# 4) One continuous piecewise prediction (NO BREAKS)
# ----------------------------
pred_piecewise <- tibble(
  day_of_water_year = seq(dec1, apr30)
) %>%
  mutate(
    fit_density = if_else(
      day_of_water_year <= mar1,
      as.numeric(predict(fit1, newdata = .)),
      as.numeric(predict(fit2, newdata = .))
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(fit_density))

# ----------------------------
# 5) Water-year month labels (Oct–Sep)
# Use month-start day numbers for a non-leap WY
# ----------------------------
month_breaks <- c(
  1,   # Oct 1
  32,  # Nov 1
  62,  # Dec 1
  93,  # Jan 1
  124, # Feb 1
  152, # Mar 1
  183, # Apr 1
  213, # May 1
  244, # Jun 1
  274, # Jul 1
  305, # Aug 1
  336  # Sep 1
)

month_labels <- c("Oct","Nov","Dec","Jan","Feb","Mar","Apr","May","Jun","Jul","Aug","Sep")

# ----------------------------
# 6) Plot
# ----------------------------
ggplot(df_0520,
       aes(x = day_of_water_year, y = snow_density,
           group = water_year, color = factor(water_year))) +

  geom_line(alpha = 0.8) +

  # Continuous fitted line (piecewise)
  geom_line(data = pred_piecewise,
            aes(x = day_of_water_year, y = fit_density, group = 1),
            inherit.aes = FALSE,
            linewidth = 1,
            color = "black") +

  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = month_breaks,
    labels = month_labels,
    limits = c(1, 365)
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Bulk Snowpack Density by Water Year (2005–2020)",
    x = "Month (Water Year: Oct–Sep)",
    y = "Bulk Snow Density (kg/m³)",
    color = "Water Year"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

# Density over the years, starting october 1
```{r, echo=FALSE}
# add a line for swe, density, and depth
# compare each new year on top of the 15 year average
# find a way to use fresh snow depth to change the average value to continue the sawtooth values even from the average
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(ggplot2)

# ----------------------------
# Constants (non-leap reference)
# ----------------------------
dec1  <- 62
mar1  <- 152
apr30 <- 212

month_breaks <- c(1, 32, 62, 93, 124, 152, 183, 213, 244, 274, 305, 336)
month_labels <- c("Oct","Nov","Dec","Jan","Feb","Mar","Apr","May","Jun","Jul","Aug","Sep")

# ----------------------------
# 1) BASELINE: build 2005–2020 piecewise trend
# ----------------------------
df_base <- df %>%
  filter(water_year >= 2005, water_year <= 2020) %>%
  filter(!is.na(snow_density)) %>%
  arrange(water_year, day_of_water_year)

avg_by_day_base <- df_base %>%
  group_by(day_of_water_year) %>%
  summarise(avg_density = mean(snow_density, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(day_of_water_year)

fit1_data <- avg_by_day_base %>%
  filter(day_of_water_year >= dec1, day_of_water_year <= mar1)

fit2_data <- avg_by_day_base %>%
  filter(day_of_water_year >= mar1, day_of_water_year <= apr30)

fit1 <- lm(avg_density ~ day_of_water_year, data = fit1_data)
fit2 <- lm(avg_density ~ day_of_water_year, data = fit2_data)

baseline_trend <- tibble(
  day_of_water_year = seq(dec1, apr30)
) %>%
  mutate(
    fit_density = if_else(
      day_of_water_year <= mar1,
      as.numeric(predict(fit1, newdata = .)),
      as.numeric(predict(fit2, newdata = .))
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(fit_density))

# ----------------------------
# 2) TARGET YEARS: 2021–2025 lines
# ----------------------------
df_2125 <- df %>%
  filter(water_year >= 2021, water_year <= 2025) %>%
  filter(!is.na(snow_density)) %>%
  arrange(water_year, day_of_water_year)

# ----------------------------
# 3) Plot 2021–2025 with 2005–2020 baseline trend overlaid
# ----------------------------
ggplot(df_2125,
       aes(x = day_of_water_year, y = snow_density,
           group = water_year, color = factor(water_year))) +

  geom_line(alpha = 0.85) +

  geom_line(data = baseline_trend,
            aes(x = day_of_water_year, y = fit_density, group = 1),
            inherit.aes = FALSE,
            linewidth = 1,
            color = "black") +

  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = month_breaks,
    labels = month_labels,
    limits = c(1, 365)
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Bulk Snowpack Density (2021–2025) with 2005–2020 Baseline Trend",
    x = "Month (Water Year: Oct–Sep)",
    y = "Bulk Snow Density (kg/m³)",
    color = "Water Year"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
